unified_narrative.one_liner: Exploring what payment infrastructure looks like when AI agents are the primary consumer, using corporate treasury as the anchor use case.
unified_narrative.core_thesis: Payments don't exist for their own sake. They serve use cases. Those use cases are becoming agentic. Google, Visa, and Mastercard are building agentic payment protocols because the consumer of payment services is shifting from humans to AI agents, and existing infrastructure wasn't designed for that. This repo explores what needs to change in the payment stack. Treasury management is the anchor use case because the decision-making layer is genuinely non-deterministic (cash positioning, FX hedging, liquidity forecasting across entities and jurisdictions), which creates a strong test of where agents add real value above the payment layer.
unified_narrative.anchor_scenario: A corporate treasurer's AI agent wakes up to: GBP receivables arriving next week (probabilistic), SGD payroll due Friday (fixed), a supplier in Lagos waiting on $10K (flexible timing), and cash scattered across entities in Singapore, London, and Delaware. The agent reasons about which entity should fund which obligation, whether to hedge the GBP/USD exposure or wait, which corridor and rail minimizes total cost for the Lagos payment, and whether to draw on the revolver or liquidate a short-term investment to cover the SGD shortfall. Then the payment infrastructure underneath executes those decisions reliably.
unified_narrative.why_this_matters_for_payments: An agent can't click 'Confirm Payment.' It needs cryptographic authorization with scoped constraints (AP2 mandates). An agent doesn't have a session cookie. It needs verifiable identity and attestation (Visa TAP). An agent operating across entities doesn't make one payment at a time. It issues batches of instructions that the infrastructure must execute reliably, with idempotency, reconciliation, and multi-rail routing. The payment stack needs to be rebuilt for this consumer.

how_projects_connect.01-mcp-payments-server: The foundation. Payment operations (initiate transfer, check status, get FX rates, sanctions screening) exposed as MCP tools that any AI agent can call. This is what 'payment infrastructure for agents' looks like at the tool level.
how_projects_connect.02-adk-payment-orchestrator: The orchestration layer. A Google ADK multi-agent system that coordinates payment workflows: compliance checks, routing decisions, FX conversion, settlement tracking. Shows how agent-native orchestration differs from traditional payment orchestration.
how_projects_connect.03-ap2-implementation: The trust layer. Working implementations of AP2, TAP, and Verifiable Intent. When an agent initiates a payment, these protocols provide cryptographic proof of authorization within scoped constraints. This is the new authentication model for agent-initiated payments.
how_projects_connect.04-cross-border-routing: The routing intelligence. Evaluates corridors and rails (SWIFT, stablecoins, mobile money, instant rails) based on real-time FX spreads, fees, and settlement speeds. Shows how multi-rail routing works when the decision-maker is an agent with access to live market data.
how_projects_connect.05-treasury-agent: The anchor use case. A treasury agent that reasons about cash positioning, FX exposure, and liquidity across entities, then drives payment instructions into the infrastructure below. Demonstrates why the payment stack needs to change: because the use cases consuming it are becoming agentic, and treasury is a strong example of genuinely non-deterministic decision-making.
how_projects_connect.06-protocol-deep-dives: The thought leadership layer. PM-level explainers for every major agentic payment protocol: what each protocol does, how it works, where it's mature, and how it fits into the stack.
how_projects_connect.07-payment-api-design: The engineering rigor. Production-grade API design with idempotency, webhook confirmations, error handling, and reconciliation. Shows that the payment infrastructure beneath the agent is built to payment-grade standards, not demo-grade.

build_philosophy.description: Working code that hits real APIs where possible (Alpha Vantage for FX, Plaid sandbox for accounts) but with mock payment backends and mock treasury data (multi-entity cash positions, forecasts). Polished enough for LinkedIn demos and GitHub engagement. The payment infrastructure projects should feel production-grade. The treasury use case should show both the deterministic path (payment execution) and the non-deterministic path (treasury reasoning) to make clear where agents add value.

projects.name: ADK Multi-Agent Payment Orchestrator
projects.description: A Google ADK multi-agent system where a root orchestrator delegates to specialized payment sub-agents (compliance, routing, FX, settlement). Demonstrates how agent-native orchestration differs from traditional payment orchestration: the agents can reason about trade-offs, handle exceptions, and coordinate across sub-problems rather than following a fixed workflow.

readme_spec.title: ADK Multi-Agent Payment Orchestrator
readme_spec.subtitle: A Google ADK multi-agent system that coordinates payment workflows through specialized sub-agents.

Quick Start (pip install google-adk, adk web)

projects.name: Cross-Border Routing Engine
projects.description: Multi-rail corridor optimization for cross-border payments. Evaluates real-time FX spreads, fee structures, and settlement speeds across SWIFT, stablecoins, mobile money, and instant rails. When an agent (treasury or otherwise) needs to move money across borders, this is the infrastructure that evaluates the options. Shows how routing intelligence works when the caller has access to live market data and can reason about trade-offs.

readme_spec.title: Cross-Border Routing Engine
readme_spec.subtitle: Multi-rail corridor optimization for agent-initiated cross-border payments.
sections: How an agent uses this (request best route for a corridor, get ranked options with costs/speeds)
Rails covered (SWIFT, stablecoins, mobile money, instant rails)

root_readme_entry.demonstrates: Multi-rail corridor optimization (SWIFT, stablecoins, mobile money)

projects.name: Treasury Agent (Anchor Use Case)
projects.description: The anchor use case that exercises the full payment stack. A treasury agent that reasons about cash positioning across multi-jurisdictional entities, FX exposure management, liquidity forecasting, and funding source selection, then drives payment instructions into the infrastructure built in Projects 01-04. Treasury is a strong use case because the decision-making is genuinely non-deterministic (that's why full-time treasurers exist), which demonstrates why payment infrastructure needs to support agent consumers. Built with LangChain, OpenAI Functions, and MCP.

readme_spec.title: Treasury Agent
readme_spec.subtitle: The anchor use case. A treasury agent that reasons about cash positioning, FX, and liquidity, then drives the payment infrastructure built in Projects 01-04.
